'''Roy Asotasi''' (born 6 January 1982) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 2000s and 2010s. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] captain, he also played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]]. Asotasi played in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for Australian clubs [[Bulldogs RLFC|Canterbury-Bankstown]] (with whom he won the [[2004 NRL Premiership]]) and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] from 2007 to 2013. He then played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Asotasi was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent,<ref name="Apia Park to add Samoa Flavour to Sevens">{{cite web|url=http://www.ciherald.co.ck/articles/h432f.htm|title=Apia Park to add Samoa Flavour to Sevens|publisher=ciherald.co.ck|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

A product of the [[Marist Saints]] junior club in [[Auckland]], he also attended [[Marcellin College, Auckland]].<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=3594580| title=League Kiwi flavour to NRL showdown| work= [[The New Zealand Herald]]| date=25 September 2004| access-date=26 October 2014| author=Jessup, Peter}}</ref>

==Professional playing career==
===Canterbury-Bankstown===
Asotasi joined Sydney [[NRL]] club, the [[Bulldogs Rugby League Football Club|Canterbury-Bankstown]] club in 2000 as a {{rlp|pr}} where he was a regular member of their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team, playing in the Premiership-winning team against [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]]. In 2001 he was again a regular member of the [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] team and played in the Premiership-winning team against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] as a {{rlp|sr}}.

By 2002 Asotasi became a regular member of the dogs of war' [[New South Wales Cup|Premier League]] team and received the opportunity to join the [[NRL|highest grade of Rugby League in Australia, the NRL]] against [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]], when [[Glen Hughes]] was injured. He returned to the Premier League to play in the Preliminary Final against [[Penrith Panthers]] but was injured and missed the Grand Final win.

In 2004, Asotasi started the season in the NRL premiership and played every game of the season including the [[2004 NRL grand final]] victory over cross-town rivals, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. In that same year he was also selected to play for New Zealand. He has played 14 Tests for New Zealand from 2004 to 2006.

As 2004 NRL premiers, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] faced [[Super League IX]] champions, [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2005 World Club Challenge]]. Asotasi played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the Bulldogs' 32–39 loss. In 2005, Asotasi played every match of the season before his sequence of 74 consecutive games was interrupted in 2006. He was injured just before the finals but returned to play in the Preliminary Final against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].

===South Sydney===
[[File:Jason taylor Not looking happy after 4 Wks of the nrl.jpg|thumb|right|Asotasi with [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]]]]
Foundation club, [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] signed Roy Asotasi for the start of the 2007 NRL season. He was selected to captain the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test Match 2007|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=1 September 2013}}</ref> With Souths he played a major part in helping the club to its first finals appearance in over two decades by finishing in seventh position.  That year Asotasi was awarded the [[George Piggins Medal]] as the club's best and fairest player for the season. Asotasi was named the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national side]] captain, as of the 2008 [[ANZAC Test|Anzac Test]] [[Test match (rugby league)|Match]]. He was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=8 August 2008|access-date=8 August 2008}}</ref>
However, in the round 25 clash against the [[Sydney Roosters]], Asotasi picked up a pectoral injury and was sidelined for the remainder of the season and subsequently, took no part in New Zealand's [[Rugby League World Cup]] success. A pectoral injury also interrupted Asotasi's 2012 season.

In April 2013, Asotasi captained [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in their test match against fierce pacific rivals [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].

===Warrington Wolves===
On 2 July 2013, Roy signed a two-year contract to play for the English Super League club [[Warrington Wolves]]. His contract began in 2014.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Roy Asotasi: Warrington Wolves sign ex-New Zealand skipper
| publisher = BBC Sport
| date = 2 July 2013
| url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/23142802
| access-date = 23 September 2015}}</ref>

On 20 August 2015, Tony Smith, coach of Warrington confirmed that he would be releasing Asotasi for the 2016 season as he wants space to sign one more player to the team next season.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Tony Smith says Warrington Wolves to sign one more, but Roy Asotasi heading back to Australia
| publisher = Warrington Guardian
| date = 20 August 2015
| url = http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/13613821.Smith_confirms_more_to_come_as_Asotasi_leaves/
| access-date = 23 September 2015}}</ref> Asotasi finished his Warrington career with 58 appearances, 5 tries and one goal.

==Highlights==
*''Junior Club:'' [[Marist Saints]]
*''Career Stats:'' 218 career games to date scoring 15 tries
* Three Premierships with the Bulldogs, 2000 and 2001 (Jersey Flegg), 2004 (NRL)
* 3 May 2007: first game played in the famous red and green.
* Appointed captain of South Sydney<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2020/01/10/captain-in-focus-roy-asotasi/ |title=Captain in Focus: Roy Asotasi |author=Brad Ryder |publisher=Rabbitohs |date=10 January 2020 |access-date=4 December 2021}}</ref>
* Captain of [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand National Rugby League Team]] (the Kiwis)<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2020/04/20/on-this-day-asotasi-captains-his-country/ |title=On This Day: Asotasi Becomes the Sixth Rabbitoh to Captain their Country |publisher=Rabbitohs |author=Lars Roy |date=20 April 2020 |access-date=4 December 2021}}</ref>
